description_blurb: This is an FBI 302 interview conducted with a US citizen regarding their first-hand account of a UAP encounter at a US test site.  USPER described an object "metallic bronze in color."
title: FBI September 2023 Sighting - Serial 3
summary: An FBI 302 interview with a US citizen regarding a first-hand account of a UAP encounter at a US test site. The witness observed a bright light, described as "metallic bronze in color" (per official blurb), that was initially stationary, then moved right, and disappeared. The witness thought it might have been a meteor.
reported_behavior: stationary in the air, then started moving to the right and then disappeared
bust_reasoning: The bust score is 75 because the document itself contains a plausible mundane explanation offered by a witness (meteor). The observation was visual only, lasted a short duration (10 seconds), and described simple kinematics (stationary, then moved right, then disappeared). While multiple witnesses were present, the corroboration was not strong, with one witness looking in the wrong direction and another being 'indifferent' until others confirmed seeing it. These factors collectively point towards a prosaic explanation being highly likely.
full_text:
# FBI September 2023 Sighting - Serial 3 - Page 1

FD-302 (Rev. 5-8-10)

- 1 of 2 -

[redacted]
FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION

[Official Record Stamp: Document participants have digitally signed. All signatures have been verified by a certified FBI information system.]

Date of entry: 10/[redacted]/2023

(U/[redacted]) On September [redacted] 2023, [redacted] and FBI Special Agent [redacted] interviewed [redacted] [redacted] [redacted] [redacted] via Facetime video ([redacted] was in [redacted] at the time of the interview). After being advised of the identity of the interviewing agents and the nature of the interview, [redacted] provided the following information:

[redacted]

(U/[redacted]) On September [redacted] 2023 at around 9:00 am, [redacted] was at [redacted] driving east to a test site to acquire data for LiDAR testing with [redacted] and [redacted] were driving an F150. [redacted] was behind them, driving a GMC AT4 with [redacted] as his passenger. [redacted] was behind them driving a sprinter van.

(U/[redacted]) The vehicles drove through a couple of gates and [redacted] saw a bright light over the horizon. The light was stationary in the air, then started moving to the right and then disappeared. [redacted] could see the light through the top right of the vehicle's windshield. The light was bright white and was visible for ten seconds before it disappeared. The light stayed the same size throughout the incident. [redacted] thought the light was ten to twenty miles away. [redacted] did not notice any interference with his vehicle.

(U/[redacted]) [redacted] pointed the light out to [redacted] but [redacted] looked in the wrong direction. [redacted] was also tall and had his seat leaned back so he was not in a good position to see the light. [redacted] was indifferent to the light until they got to the first test site and [redacted] and [redacted] said they saw it too.

[redacted]

Investigation on 09/[redacted]/2023 at [redacted] United States (Other (Facetime Video))
File # [redacted]
Date drafted 10/[redacted]/2023
by [redacted]

This document contains neither recommendations nor conclusions of the FBI. It is the property of the FBI and is loaned to your agency; it and its contents are not to be distributed outside your agency.



# FBI September 2023 Sighting - Serial 3 - Page 2

FD-302a (Rev. 5-8-10)

[redacted] / [redacted]

[redacted] [redacted] September 2023 [redacted] UAP
Continuation of FD-302 of [redacted] , On 09/[redacted]/2023 , Page 2 of 2

[redacted] thought the light might have been a meteor coming straight toward them and burning up in the atmosphere.

[redacted] / [redacted]
cover_up_reasoning: The cover-up score is 30 because the document contains routine redactions typical of an FBI report released via FOIA. These redactions primarily involve Personally Identifiable Information (PII) such as names of witnesses and FBI agents, specific dates, locations, and internal file numbers. There is no evidence of classified annexes, extensive sentence-level redactions obscuring the narrative, or contradictions with public statements.
title_zh: 联邦调查局2023年9月目击事件 - 序列3
summary_zh: 联邦调查局对一名美国公民进行302号访谈，内容涉及其在美国某试验场亲身遭遇UAP的报告。目击者观察到一个亮光，据官方描述为“金属青铜色”，该物体最初静止，随后向右移动并消失。目击者推测其可能为一颗流星。
reported_behavior_zh: 在空中静止，随后开始向右移动并消失
bust_reasoning_zh: 破解评分为75，原因在于该文件本身包含目击者提出的一个合理世俗解释（流星）。此次观察仅为视觉观察，持续时间短（10秒），且描述了简单的运动学特征（静止，随后向右移动，然后消失）。尽管有多名目击者在场，但相互印证不力，其中一名目击者看向错误方向，另一名目击者在他人确认看到之前表现“漠然”。这些因素共同表明，存在世俗解释的可能性极高。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 掩盖评分为30，原因在于该文件包含通过《信息自由法案》发布的联邦调查局报告中常见的例行编辑。这些编辑主要涉及个人身份信息（PII），例如目击者和联邦调查局探员的姓名、具体日期、地点以及内部档案编号。没有证据表明存在机密附件、大量句子级别的编辑模糊叙述，或与公开声明相矛盾之处。
claims: Object was stationary, then moved right, then disappeared
Object was visible for approximately ten seconds
Witness considered the possibility it was a meteor
claims_zh: 物体最初静止，随后向右移动并消失
目击者考虑其可能为流星
bust_explanations: Meteor/Fireball: A witness explicitly suggested the object 'might have been a meteor coming straight toward them and burning up in the atmosphere.' The description of a bright light visible for ten seconds, moving and then disappearing, is consistent with a meteor, despite the initial 'stationary' perception which could be due to distance or initial observation angle.

Distant Aircraft or Satellite Flare: A distant aircraft reflecting sunlight could appear as a bright, stationary light that then moves and disappears as its angle to the observer changes or it moves out of sight. Similarly, a satellite flare (e.g., from a tumbling satellite or space debris) could produce a brief, bright light that appears, moves, and then fades or disappears.

High-altitude Balloon or Drone: A high-altitude balloon or a large drone could appear as a bright, stationary object reflecting sunlight, then move to the right, and eventually disappear from view. The 'bright white' description fits sunlight reflection off such an object.
bust_explanations_zh: 流星/火球：一名目击者明确表示，该物体“可能是一颗流星，径直朝他们飞来并在大气层中燃烧殆尽”。尽管最初感知为“静止”（这可能由于距离或初始观察角度），但对亮光可见十秒、移动随后消失的描述与流星现象一致。
远距离飞机或卫星闪光：一架反射阳光的远距离飞机可能表现为明亮的静止光点，随后随着其与观察者的角度变化或移出视线而移动并消失。类似地，卫星闪光（例如，来自翻滚卫星或空间碎片）也可能产生短暂的亮光，出现、移动随后逐渐变暗或消失。
高空氦气球或无人机：一个高空氦气球或大型无人机可能表现为反射阳光的明亮静止物体，随后向右移动，并最终从视野中消失。“亮白色”的描述符合此类物体反射阳光的特征。
cover_up_indicators: Redaction of specific dates (day of month)
Redaction of FBI internal file numbers
cover_up_indicators_zh: 联邦调查局探员姓名被编辑
具体日期（月份中的某一天）被编辑
联邦调查局内部档案编号被编辑
